data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's February revenue surges 43% year-on-year, falls on a monthly basis
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (NYSE:TSM) revenue for February 2024 jumped 43% year-over-year to NT$260.01B, amid growing demand for AI applications.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and AMD (AMD) — saw revenue for the January-February grow about 39% year-over-year to around NT$553.30B. That compares with 34% growth during the full year of 2024.

However, on a consolidated basis, TSMC saw a decrease of 11.3% in revenue compared to a month ago. 

TSMC’s sales are a barometer for the semiconductor sector as the company manufactures most of the AI chips in the world, and the numbers come amid heated competition in the AI landscape, despite uncertainties.

TSM Chief Executive C.C Wei said on the company's earnings call that U.S. export controls on AI chips were a manageable issue for the company.

The company is also set to invest $100B over the next few years in U.S. semiconductor manufacturing, including the establishment of new chip fabs and research facilities. 

President Donald Trump has meanwhile called for ending a bipartisan $52 billion semiconductor subsidy program that’s spurred more than $400 billion in investments from companies like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and Intel Corp.

Elsewhere, TSMC rival Intel’s (INTC) $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to be completed only by 2030.

data.title: ASE Technology February revenue rose 8.3% Y/Y to $1.37B
Parade Technologies acquires Spectra7 majority assets
HIVE Digital mines 89 BTC in February, Bitcoin holdings up by 23% Y/Y
data.description:
HIVE Digital Technologies (HIVE) announced February 2025 production results, mining 89 BTC (BTC-USD) and maintaining a HODL portfolio of 2,620 BTC valued at $220 million as of February 28, 2025, based on a BTC closing price of approximately $84,000.

The company's Bitcoin holdings increased 23% year-over-year, though decreased month-over-month due to strategic BTC sales for capital investments.

The company maintained an average mining capacity of 5.6 Exahash per Second with 20.9 J/TH fleet efficiency, achieving a peak BTC-only hashrate of 6.2 EH/s.

The company achieved daily production averaged 3.2 BTC, equivalent to 16 BTC per Exahash.

"Our 300 MW expansion in Paraguay to 4x our hashrate from 6 EH/s to 25 EH/s by September 2025 remains on track. We expect to complete final construction and phased energization of miners for the first 100 MWs at Yguazú and 100 MW at Valenzuela in calendar Q2, 2025, which will bring us to an estimated interim target of 18 EH/s and 18 J/TH by June 30, 2025," said Aydin Kilic, President and CEO.

Additionally, HIVE's GPU business unit achieved $13 million annualized run-rate revenue in late February, targeting $20 million by Q2 2025, he added.

data.title: India's Sun Pharma buys onco-derma firm Checkpoint Therapeutics for $355M
data.description:
India's pharma giant Sun Pharma to acquire Checkpoint Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CKPT)—an immunotherapy and targeted oncology company—for an upfront cash payment of $355M.

The deal involves an upfront cash payment of $4.10 per share, representing up to $355M, a 66% premium to Checkpoint's (NASDAQ:CKPT) closing share price on March 7, 2025. CKPT stockholders will also receive a contingent value right for up to $0.70 per share on achievement of milestones.

Transaction closing is expected in Q2 2025.

CheckPoint is a commercial-stage company focused on developing novel treatments for patients with solid tumor cancers. It reported revenues of $0.04M and a net loss of $27.3M for the nine-month period ending September 2024.

This acquisition aims to strengthen Sun Pharma's global onco-dermatology franchise by adding Checkpoint's FDA-approved treatment, UNLOXCYT (cosibelimab-ipdl), to its portfolio.

"Combining UNLOXCYT with Sun Pharma’s global presence means patients with cSCC may soon have access to an important, new treatment option. The acquisition further bolsters our innovative portfolio in onco-derm therapy," said Dilip Shanghvi, Chairman & Managing Director of Sun Pharma.

In connection with the transaction, Checkpoint (CKPT), Sun Pharma, and Fortress Biotech (NASDAQ:FBIO)—Checkpoint's controlling stockholder—have entered into a royalty agreement under which, following the closing of the transaction, Fortress (NASDAQ:FBIO) would be entitled to receive royalty payments based on future sales of cosibelimab during a specified term.

FBIO (FBIO) stock price traded 3.8% higher on Monday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Asia markets mixed in response to weak Chinese CPI and PPI data; tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets mixed on Monday as traders continued to closely monitor developments in tariff talks amid a lack of domestic market catalysts. China’s retaliatory tariffs on US farm goods took effect, following the US hiking tariffs on Chinese goods from 10% to 20%.

Traders reacted to fresh data from China over the weekend, which showed that consumer prices fell the most in 13 months in February, while producer deflation persisted for the 29th month despite a slower fall.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.42% to around 3,990, while the broader Topix Index edged up 0.1% to 2,712 on Monday, recovering some losses from the previous session. The Japanese yen strengthened past 148 per dollar on Monday, hovering at a five-month high.

Domestically, data showed that Japan posted a current account deficit in January for the first time in two years, as imports outpaced exports. Additionally, inflation-adjusted real wages—a key measure of consumer purchasing power—fell 1.8% year-on-year, marking the first decline in three months.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.62% to below 3,360, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.3% to 10,810 on Monday, extending losses from the previous session as persistent deflationary pressures underscored China's economic challenges, and the offshore yuan depreciated around 7.25 per dollar as investors reacted to China’s weaker-than-expected inflation data.

China announced on Saturday that it will impose a 100% tariff on imports of certain Canadian agricultural products, including rapeseed oil, oil cakes, and peas, along with a 25% levy on aquatic products and pork. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 2.29% to 23,888 on Monday morning, extending losses from the prior period amid widespread sector falls.

Locally, Hong Kong's foreign exchange reserves fell to an 8-month low in February, following a period of stability over the previous three months. 

India (SENSEX) rose 0.40% to 74,601 in early deals on Monday, hitting a near two-week peak, with the Nifty 50 climbing 0.4% to above 22,600, after being flat in the previous session, supported by strength in consumer goods, metals, and realty.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.18% to around 7,970 on Monday, recovering some losses from the previous session, as mining and energy stocks led gains amid firmer commodity prices. The Australian dollar steadied around $0.63 on Monday, pausing a two-day decline.

In the U.S., on Friday, all three major indexes ended higher recovered from earlier losses after Fed Chair Jerome Powell reiterated confidence in inflation progress while emphasizing no urgency to cut rates. 

On Sunday, US President Donald Trump described the economy as undergoing “a period of transition” during a Fox News interview, when asked about the risk of a recession.

U.S. stock futures declined on Monday ahead of the inflation and JOLTS data: Dow -0.43%; S&P 500 -0.46%; Nasdaq -0.51%.

Markets are closely watching consumer inflation expectations set for release on Monday, followed by consumer inflation data on Wednesday, producer inflation figures on Thursday, and a consumer sentiment report on Friday. 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China’s CPI fell for first time in 13 months, PPI deflation persists
  • China's trade surplus surpass forecasts, exports grow by 2.3% and imports shrink
  • Australia’s trade balance exceeds expectations, export growth accelerates and imports decline
  • Australia’s economy grows by 0.6% in Q4, surpasses expectations
  • Japan’s services sector grows at fastest pace in six months, driven by strong demand

data.title: China’s CPI fell for first time in 13 months, PPI deflation persists
data.description:
China's consumer prices fell 0.7% year-on-year in February, a 13-month low, exceeding the expected 0.5% drop and reversing January’s 0.5% rise. Producer deflation also continued, down 2.2% year-on-year, compared with market forecasts of a 2.1% decline and after a 2.3% drop in the prior two months, though at its slowest pace since August 2024. 

China's food prices fell 3.3% year-over-year in February 2025, swinging from a 0.4% increase in the previous month and marking the first decline this year.

Monthly, the CPI fell 0.2%, shifting from January's 11-month top of a 0.7% rise and marking the first drop since last November. This fall was also steeper than consensus of a 0.1% decrease.

Meanwhile, despite Beijing's recent political meeting emphasizing economic growth support, persistent weak demand and escalating US trade tensions continue to create uncertainty. China's new tariffs on U.S. farm products are now in effect, and further trade disputes are anticipated with the upcoming 100% tariffs on select Canadian agricultural goods, a direct response to U.S. tariff threats.

China announced on Saturday that it will impose a 100% tariff on imports of certain Canadian agricultural products, including rapeseed oil, oil cakes, and peas, along with a 25% levy on aquatic products and pork.

The Shanghai Composite fell 0.4% to below 3,360, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.3% to 10,810 on Monday, extending losses from the previous session as persistent deflationary pressures underscored China's economic challenges, and the offshore yuan depreciated around 7.25 per dollar as investors reacted to China’s weaker-than-expected inflation data.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: 'Mickey 17' knocks 'Captain America' from top spot at box office
data.description:
The science fiction movie “Mickey 17” with Robert Pattinson opened in first place at the North American weekend box office, ending the three-week run of Marvel’s “Captain America: Brave New World” in the top spot. 

“Mickey 17” brought in $19.1M, on top of the $34.2 million it earned in overseas release. 

The movie, which reportedly cost $118M to produce, was originally supposed to be released a year ago but was delayed due to the Hollywood strikes in 2023. 

“Captain America” came in second, with an $8.5M haul. “Last Breath” made $4.2M, while “The Monkey” and “Paddington in Peru” each earned $3.9M. 

According to Comscore, the top movies at the box office between Friday and Sunday were:  

1. “Mickey 17,” $19.1 million.

2. “Captain America: Brave New World,” $8.5 million.

3. “Last Breath,” $4.2 million.

4. “The Monkey,” $3.9 million.

5. “Paddington in Peru,” $3.9 million.

6. “Dog Man,” $3.5 million.

7. “Anora,” $1.9 million.

8. “Mufasa: The Lion King,” $1.7 million.

9. “Rule Breakers,” $1.6 million.

10. “In the Lost Land,” $1 million.

data.title: Mark Carney to replace Justin Trudeau as Canada's prime minister
data.description:
Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after he was elected leader of Canada’s Labor Party on the first round of voting at the party’s convention on Sunday. 

Carney will succeed Justin Trudeau, who has led the country since November 2015.

Carney, who has never held elected office, has served as governor of the Bank of Canada and the Bank of England. 

In winning the party vote, Carney beat out former Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, former Cabinet member Karina Gould, and former MP Frank Baylis.

Canada's next federal election is required to be held by late October. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
